title: night: align page copy and flyer with current event
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## What changed

- align the Night page extras, RSVP wording, form option, metadata, and worker route metadata with the current flyer
- replace the direct flyer and 4:5 flyer endpoints with the current event flyer

## Why

The page still described a separate personalized-cocktail item and used the more aggressive "hate for AI" wording. Its social preview image was also the prior flyer.

## Validation

`npm run build --prefix pollinations.ai`

gist.headline: Night gets the right flyer
gist.blurb: The Night page and its little corners now all point at the same party. RSVP copy, event details, and social previews have stopped living in separate timelines.
gist.summary: Updated the Night page’s extras, RSVP wording, form option, metadata, worker route metadata, and both flyer image endpoints to match the current event flyer.
gist.impact: Visitors and shared links now see consistent current-event details and the updated flyer across the Night page, social preview, and 4:5 image routes.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy nighttime event noticeboard where Polly helps replace an older poster with the current Night flyer, while a friendly CRT robot checks that matching RSVP cards and social-preview frames are aligned. Use paper flyers, a small garden vine around the board, and warm lantern light to represent consistent page copy and metadata.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, a cute pixel bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, black antennae and oval eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, a happy smile, and rounded light-blue translucent wings, wearing a tiny event lanyard as she pins a fresh Night flyer onto a garden noticeboard; a chunky CRT monitor robot holds matching RSVP cards and preview frames nearby. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, code-vines and pixel flowers, warm lantern lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, with simple pixel-font text: "NIGHT".
